item.title: Architecture
item.description: Chroxy is a self-hosted daemon that bridges remote clients to a local AI coding CLI.

item.title: Configuration
item.description: Chroxy resolves configuration from multiple sources with a clear precedence order.

item.title: Container Isolation
item.description: Chroxy supports three levels of session isolation, from lightweight sandboxing to full Docker containers, so you can constrain what an AI session can touch on the host.

item.title: Developer Guide
item.description: This page covers running Chroxy locally, the repo layout, and how to extend it. For the system overview see Architecture.

item.title: Chroxy Documentation
item.description: Chroxy is a remote terminal and chat front-end for AI coding CLIs.

item.title: Providers
item.description: Chroxy runs AI coding sessions through pluggable providers.

item.title: Security & Encryption
item.description: Chroxy is self-hosted: the server daemon runs on the user’s own development machine, and remote clients connect to it through a Cloudflare tunnel.

item.title: Skills
item.description: Skills are reusable instruction snippets that Chroxy injects into every session you start.

item.title: Overnight maintenance session — 2026-06-06
item.description: Overnight maintenance session — 2026-06-06 Autonomous, self-paced run kicked off before bed. Mode: aggressive.
